D3 Chemistry L5 Evaluate Reference

Spectral Paint Mixing — Add K and S, Not RGB

two paints mix not by averaging RGB but by combining K(λ) and S(λ); the result respects spectral physics and reproduces real painter's surprises (mixing blue + yellow → grey, not green)

For two pigments at mass fractions x₁, x₂: K_mix(λ) = x₁K₁ + x₂K₂, S_mix(λ) = x₁S₁ + x₂S₂, R∞ from the K-M equation. Naive RGB averaging cannot reproduce this — it gives muddy interpolations that ignore where each pigment actually absorbs. Compare the two side by side.

Drag the fraction. Two pigment swatches sit at the ends; the K-M prediction (left) and a naive RGB average (right) bracket the slider position. They diverge most for complementary or strongly-coloured pairs.
